channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Court sentences Umar Hayat to death in Sana Yousaf murder case
item.description: ISLAMABAD: In a major development in the tragic case of social media influencer Sana Yousaf, a local Islamabad court has handed down the death penalty to Umar Hayat, the main accused in her murder. Th...

item.title: Major Health, Social, and Political Developments Across Africa
item.description: On 19th May 2026, several significant events unfolded across Africa. In the Democratic Republic of the Congo, an Ebola outbreak caused by the Bundibugyo virus has resulted in at least 131 deaths, with...

item.title: DILG Confirms No Evidence of Attack on Senate
item.description: The Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) has clarified that, based on all available evidence, there was no attack on the Philippine Senate. DILG Chief emphasized that authorities hav...

item.title: Adani Group shares gain following US dropping charges against Gautam Adani
item.description: The Adani Group witnessed a significant surge in its stock prices after the United States authorities dropped all charges against its chairman, Gautam Adani. Investors responded positively to this dev...

item.title: Andy Burnham: Evaluating the Progressive Credentials of Labour’s Leading Candidate
item.description: Owen Jones examines the prospects of Andy Burnham as a potential leader for the Labour Party, arguing that while he is Labour’s most popular figure and could reclaim votes lost to the Greens and Refor...

item.title: UK job market contracts amid rising energy prices and Iran conflict
item.description: In April 2026, British employers significantly reduced hiring, with payroll data showing a drop of 100,000 jobs from March. The Office for National Statistics confirmed this is the largest monthly dec...

item.title: Nation Faces Transport Standstill as Fuel Price Protests Continue
item.description: On Tuesday, Kenyans across major towns continued to walk long distances to work after public transport ground to a halt due to a strike over rising fuel prices. The paralysis affected Nairobi, Nakuru,...

item.title: Lorry Driver Jailed for 11 Years After Causing Fatal Crash While on Video Call
item.description: A lorry driver from Croatia, Zvonko Tomisa, 58, has been sentenced to 11 years in prison after causing a fatal motorway collision in Kent. On 25 November last year, Tomisa was driving a MAN lorry from...

item.title: How Canadians Can Navigate Travel Challenges Amid Rising Costs and Flight Disruptions
item.description: This summer, Canadians planning to travel abroad face a range of challenges, from rising fuel costs to flight cancellations and potential boycotts of U.S. destinations. The ongoing U.S.-Israel conflic...

item.title: Behind-the-Scenes Polaroids of Drew Barrymore as Dylan Sanders During Charlie’s Angels Filming in the Early 2000s
item.description: In the early 2000s, while filming the Charlie’s Angels movie franchise, makeup artist Kim Greene took a series of candid Polaroid photos of Drew Barrymore. These images capture the behind-the-scenes p...

item.title: Conservation challenges: Protecting orangutans amid expanding palm oil plantations
item.description: In Kalimantan, Indonesian Borneo, the expansion of palm oil plantations and resettlement schemes has led to increasing encounters between humans and wild orangutans. Families like Edi Ramli’s, relocat...

item.title: Joburg Ballet fundraiser links art and dance to support young dancers
item.description: Joburg Ballet in Johannesburg has launched an innovative fundraising initiative to support its Pointe Shoe fund, aimed at providing young, talented ballet dancers with the essential footwear needed fo...

item.title: Ontario Tourist Destination Prepares for Summer and Off-Season Visitor Growth
item.description: Ontario’s popular tourist destination is gearing up for what is expected to be a busy summer season. Local authorities and business owners are implementing measures to accommodate the influx of visito...

item.title: AFP Reports Fewer Chinese Vessels in West Philippine Sea This Week
item.description: The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) reported monitoring a total of 27 Chinese vessels in key areas of the West Philippine Sea from May 12 to 18, 2026. This number is lower compared to 35 vessels...

item.title: Leah Purcell Reimagines The Drover’s Wife Through Opera, Highlighting Indigenous Stories and Colonial Histories
item.description: Leah Purcell has expanded the story of The Drover’s Wife from its origins in Henry Lawson’s 1892 short story into a multidimensional work spanning play, novel, film and now opera. Lawson's original st...

item.title: Elon Musk’s Lawsuit Against OpenAI Dismissed, Key Legal Questions Unresolved
item.description: On 18 May 2026, a federal jury in Oakland, California swiftly dismissed Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI and its CEO Sam Altman. The jury’s decision, reached in under two hours, focused solely on pr...

item.title: LSK Backs Nairobi OCS Over Release of Protesters Amid Fuel Protests
item.description: The Law Society of Kenya (LSK) has come out strongly in support of Chief Inspector Dishen Ongoya, the Officer Commanding Station (OCS) at Nairobi Central Police, after he was arrested for releasing 64...

item.title: UDA Secretary-General Defends Ruto Amid Matatu Protests Over Fuel Prices
item.description: UDA Secretary-General Hassan Omar has addressed ongoing protests against President William Ruto, calling on Kenyans to exercise patience and wait for the 2027 elections if they are unhappy with his le...

item.title: Yanga’s Premier League title hopes influenced by Azam FC results
item.description: Yanga SC, one of Tanzania’s top football clubs, is facing a challenging season as they try to defend their Premier League title. The team’s success this season largely depends on how Azam FC performs ...

item.title: Content Creator 2Mbili Shares Details of Rachel Wandeto's Final Moments and Family Struggles Before Passing
item.description: Content creator 2Mbili has opened up about the tough last days of gospel singer Rachel Wandeto, who passed away at Kenyatta National Hospital after suffering severe burns from a brutal attack in Mwiki...
